articles.title: TTC Line 2 reopens after fire disrupts service
articles.summary: A fire briefly shut down a portion on one of Toronto’s subway lines on Tuesday. Shortly after 4 p.m., the TTC announced that service on Line 2 Bloor-Danforth was closed between St. George and Broadview stations due to a fire. Shuttle buses were sent to transport passangers. Service on Line 2 reopened just before 4:30 […]

articles.title: Toronto man, 37, charged with manslaughter in opioid overdose
articles.summary: A man from Toronto is facing charges for manslaughter in connection with the overdose of a 37-year-old man in St. Catharines earlier this year. Officers with the Niagara Regional Police Service were called to a home in the area of Lloyd Street and Rykert Street on January 28, 2026, for reports of a sudden death. […]

articles.title: Crown, defence propose maximum youth sentence for 15-year-old who killed beloved Pickering senior
articles.summary: WARNING: Details of this story may be disturbing to some readers Crown and defence lawyers have made a joint submission to an Ontario court arguing that a teen who pleaded guilty to the first-degree murder of a beloved 83-year-old woman outside of her Pickering home in 2025 should face the maximum youth sentence. During a […]

articles.title: 2 injured, 1 critically, in house fire near Kensington Market
articles.summary: A two‑alarm house fire near Kensington Market sent two people to the hospital on Tuesday afternoon, Toronto police said. The fire broke out around 12:27 p.m. at a two-storey residence in the area of Bathurst Street and Dundas Street West. When fire crews arrived they found smoke billowing out the second floor windows and two […]

articles.title: Teen who fatally stabbed elderly woman in Pickering in 2025 apologizes in court
articles.summary: A 15-year-old boy who fatally stabbed a retired kindergarten teacher in Pickering last year apologized in court on Tuesday.

articles.title: ‘Looking forward:’ Bonnie Crombie officially enters race for Mississauga mayor
articles.summary: Former Mississauga Mayor Bonnie Crombie has officially entered the race to get her old job back. Crombie left the mayor’s office in 2024 to become the leader of the Ontario Liberal Party. She ran in the 2025 provincial election in Mississauga East—Cooksville and lost, later resigning as Liberal leader in Jan. 2026. Now she’s back. […]

articles.title: Police searching for suspect after ‘violent’ sexual assault near Whitby park
articles.summary: The Durham Regional Police Service (DRPS) are searching for a male suspect after a woman was violently sexually assaulted late Monday night along a popular waterfront trail in Whitby. Officers were called to Iroquois Beach Park, near Gordon Street and the Waterfront Trail, shortly after 10:15 p.m. for reports that a woman had been attacked […]

articles.title: 2 men injured in collision involving vehicle and e-bike in North York
articles.summary: Toronto police say two men suffered serious injuries after a collision involving a vehicle and an e‑bike in North York early on Tuesday. The crash occurred around 5:20 a.m. at the intersection of Leslie Street and Adra Grado Way, near Sheppard Avenue East. Police said emergency crews received reports of a vehicle striking an e‑bike […]

articles.title: Motorcyclist killed in Mississauga collision, Peel police say
articles.summary: The fatal collision happened just before 10 p.m. Sunday near Argentia and Kitimat roads, police say.

articles.title: Marina Mabrey scores 30 as Tempo hold off Liberty in Montreal, ending 4-game skid
articles.summary: The Toronto Tempo held off a late-game charge from the New York Liberty on Sunday, winning 93-91 to end a four-game losing streak in their second of two games at the Bell Centre in Montreal.

articles.title: Bonnie Crombie announces intention to run for mayor of Mississauga
articles.summary: After a short stint leading the Ontario Liberals, Bonnie Crombie is looking to get her old job back as mayor of Mississauga, Ont.

articles.title: 64-year-old woman dead after being struck by dump truck in Ajax: police
articles.summary: Durham police say a 64-year-old woman is dead after being struck by a dump truck in Ajax on Saturday.

articles.title: Man, 68, dead after being struck by vehicle in Pickering parking lot: Durham police
articles.summary: Durham police say a 68-year-old man is dead after being struck by a vehicle in a Pickering parking lot Saturday.
